items.title: Oil: Prices slump below $100 on ceasefire – ING
items.summary: ING’s commodities team of Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson report that Oil prices have dropped sharply, with Brent and WTI falling well below $100 as a US–Iran two‑week ceasefire eases supply disruption fears.

items.title: USD: Relief-driven downside potential – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that the US-Iran ceasefire has pushed financial markets into relief mode, with Brent, equities and bonds reacting positively and the Dollar falling sharply.

items.title: UK: PMI inflation surge clouds outlook – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Head of Research Derek Halpenny underlines that United Kingdom (UK) PMI Services and Composite data show a larger downturn than in Europe, with a record jump in input prices driven by energy concerns.

items.title: NZD/USD rallies to near 0.5850 amid risk-on mood, RBNZ’s hawkish remarks
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is up 1.6% to near 0.5830 in the European trading session on Wednesday.

items.title: Euro steadies at five-week highs near 1.1700 amid market optimism
items.summary: The announcement of a two-week ceasefire in Iran triggered a relief rally during Wednesday’s Asian trading session that sent the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) tumbling against its main peers.

items.title: US President Trump: Talking tariff and sanctions relief with Iran
items.summary: United States (US) President Donald Trump said on Truth Social on Wednesday that the US will work closely with Iran through a "very productive regime change."

items.title: NZD: RBNZ tightening bias and earlier hike risk – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities analysts review the latest Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) decision, noting the cash rate was held at 2.25% while inflation forecasts were revised sharply higher for Q1 and Q2.

items.title: Dow Jones futures rise as US-Iran ceasefire lifts market sentiment
items.summary: Dow Jones futures rise 2.32% to near 47,900 during European hours on Wednesday, ahead of the regular United States (US) open. Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures also gain 2.49% and 3.19% to near 6,820 and 25,150, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: NZD/USD: RBNZ hawkish nuance but Kiwi capped – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur reports that the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) left rates unchanged, but Governor Breman’s comments about discussing a hike were read as slightly hawkish, supporting the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) against the US Dollar (USD).

items.title: USD: Market reprieve and Dollar risks – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank strategists Michael Every and Bas van Geffen note that a temporary ceasefire between the United States (US) and Iran has sharply reduced immediate risk premia, with Brent lower and equities higher. However, they stress this is only a short truce, leaving at least two weeks of uncertainty.

items.title: S&P 500: Futures rebound on Hormuz ceasefire – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank analysts report a strong recovery in S&P 500 futures after the US–Iran two-week ceasefire announcement and easing war fears. Futures are now less than 2% below pre-strike levels and well above the late-March lows.

items.title: Australian Dollar jumps on US–Iran ceasefire, traders await FOMC Minutes
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair climbs to around 0.7080, the highest since March 20, during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) edges higher against the Greenback as the ceasefire between the US and Iran triggers a broad risk-on sentiment.
